nodes.title: Normalize case and remove noise
nodes.why: This makes comparisons fair by ignoring case and punctuation.
nodes.tradeoff: Normalization uses extra memory, but gives the expected behavior for real inputs.

nodes.title: Use pointers from both ends
nodes.why: Palindromes are symmetric, so checking mirrored positions is direct and efficient.
nodes.alternative: Reverse the string and compare it with the original.
nodes.tradeoff: Two pointers avoid building another full string for comparison logic.

nodes.title: Scan only until pointers cross
nodes.why: Once pointers meet, all mirrored pairs have been checked.
nodes.tradeoff: Middle-stop is faster and clearer than scanning unnecessary positions.

nodes.title: Return false on first mismatch
nodes.why: One mismatch is enough to prove the string is not a palindrome.
nodes.alternative: Track a flag and finish the whole loop.
nodes.tradeoff: Fail-fast is simpler and usually faster on non-palindromes.

nodes.title: Return true after full symmetric check
nodes.why: If no mismatches were found, the normalized string is a palindrome.
nodes.tradeoff: Explicit `true` keeps the success path easy to read.
